description: Documented compromises of GitHub Actions, classes of weak action-usage patterns, and CI/CD pipeline supply-chain incidents.

entries.name: tj-actions/changed-files
entries.description: Attackers compromised the tj-actions/changed-files action and pushed a version that exfiltrated all workflow secrets to a public attacker endpoint. ~23,000 repositories pulled the malicious tag before remediation. Every consumer using a floating tag (e.g. @v1) was impacted.

entries.name: reviewdog/action-setup
entries.description: Same attack chain as tj-actions: attacker pushed a compromised reviewdog/action-setup release that exfiltrated CI secrets. Tag-pin consumers were impacted; SHA-pin consumers were not.

entries.name: codecov-bash-uploader-action
entries.description: Codecov's `bash <(curl https://codecov.io/bash)` uploader (often invoked from custom GitHub Actions) was modified on its CDN to exfiltrate env vars between Jan 31 and Apr 1, 2021. Any Action that piped the uploader script was affected.

entries.name: ultralytics-actions
entries.description: Compromised GitHub Actions cache + release workflow allowed injection of a Python wheel that exfiltrated env vars and mined Monero. Affected PyPI release path of the Ultralytics YOLO package.

entries.name: actions/checkout@untrusted-ref
entries.description: Pattern weakness: workflow_run / pull_request_target combined with `actions/checkout` using `${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}` checks out attacker-controlled code with secrets in the environment. Documented by GitHub Security Lab as `pwn requests`.

entries.name: peter-evans/create-or-update-comment-injection
entries.description: Actions that interpolate untrusted text into `body:` parameter without escaping are vulnerable to GitHub Actions script injection. Pattern flagged across many community actions; create-or-update-comment hardened in 3.1.0.

entries.name: ericcornelissen/git-tag-annotation-action
entries.description: Demonstration / disclosure that a single re-pushed tag on a third-party action could distribute malicious code to all downstream consumers using floating tags. The maintainer remediated immediately and published a post-mortem.

entries.name: dawidd6/action-download-artifact-injection
entries.description: Actions that download artifacts from arbitrary workflow runs without verifying provenance can be tricked into pulling attacker-controlled artifacts. Hardening guidance now requires verifying source workflow + commit SHA before extracting.
